title.description: A friendly title of the task

type.description: One of the known types of available task types. Specifies the type of task to run.

summary.description: A short summary for the task in the [Subject] [Action-er] format, under four words, e.g., "Laptop Finder", "Meal Planner"

objective.description: Specifies the objective for the task. Must include all information necessary to successfully complete the task.

slug.description: A single directory name for the task's working area (e.g., 'research'). The system automatically nests this under your own working directory if you are a subagent.

options.description: Optional runtime configuration overrides for the task as key-value pairs. Supported keys and values depend entirely on the task type being created. Discover available options for each task type by calling 'tasks_list_types'.

task_id.description: The ID of the task to cancel.

name: tasks_send_event
description: Send a typed event to a child task's agent. The agent receives the event as a context update. Use this to provide additional instructions, clarifications, or data to a running subagent.

task_id.description: The ID of the task to send the event to.

type.description: The type of event (e.g., 'clarification', 'additional_context'). Choose descriptive, stable names.

message.description: Text payload delivered to the child agent. Include all relevant details the agent needs to act on.

name: tasks_await
description: Suspend execution until a context update arrives (e.g. a child task completes or a parent sends an event). Returns immediately if updates are already pending. Call this after creating tasks to wait for their results.

task_ids.description: Optional. Task IDs to wait for. Currently informational — the function resumes on any context update.
